title: eriophorum angustifolium subsp. subarcticum
intro: Eriophorum angustifolium subsp. subarcticum, also known as the subarctic cotton-grass, is a subspecies of the Eriophorum angustifolium plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The subarctic cotton-grass has been reported to offer various health benefits, such as wound healing, anti-cancer properties, and immune system support. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, with some herbalists also using it in salves and creams for topical applications.
